element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Rogue Encampment tutorial area with weak monsters
Wilderness and forest encounters before dungeon delving
Early boss encounters and moderate monster density
Monastery and wilderness areas with branching paths
Desert enemies with fire/cold attacks in Egyptian-themed zones
Nightmare and Hell difficulties where immunity becomes critical
Mid-game loot progression via normal, magical, rare, set, unique tier system
Full replay with stronger monster variations and resistances
Extreme monster strength and item resistance requirements
Optional mode for experienced players seeking maximum difficulty

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching town/safe zone (Rogue Encampment, Lut Gholein, Kurast, Harrogath)
Transitioning between acts with new thematic areas
typical_tension_triggers: Descending into dungeons with unknown layouts
Encountering monsters with resistances matching player weaknesses
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Quest-driven progression with escalating monster difficulty across five acts; tension builds through random generation and resistance mechanics, relieved by town hubs and completed objectives; intensity amplifies through three sequential difficulty replays

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side dungeons accessible from main paths provide optional monster encounters and experience rewards

difficulty_curve.early_game: Act I introduces basic combat in protected Encampment, gradually exposing players to wilderness randomization and modest dungeon complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Acts II-III escalate through exotic environments (Egyptian desert, Central American jungle) with elemental enemies introducing resistance requirements and item diversity necessity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Act IV/V culminates in Hell setting with heavily resistant monsters; Nightmare and Hell replays compound difficulty through stat penalization and immune monsters
notable_spikes: Act I boss (Andariel) first major test
Nightmare difficulty monsters with multiple immunities
Hell difficulty extreme monster variations and player handicaps

notable_level_patterns.description: Each act centers on a town (safe zone with NPCs, merchants, waypoints) radiating outward to wilderness and dungeons with predetermined objectives and quest markers, creating progression funnels
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Act I: Rogue Encampment → Wilderness → Monastery/Barracks/Catacombs

notable_level_patterns.description: Wilderness and dungeons between key locations regenerate each playthrough while maintaining visual/environmental theme (forests in Act I, deserts in Act II, jungles in Act III)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Diablo's Stony Tomb, Lost City, Kurast Temples

notable_level_patterns.description: Visual and mechanical contrast between acts (boreal forests → Egyptian tombs → jungle temples → Hell → mountains) prevents monotony and signals progression narrative
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Act I boreal vs Act IV lava/cathedral Hell

notable_level_patterns.description: Extra dungeons branch from main paths, offering risk/reward for monster slaying and loot gathering without blocking story progression
Completing all five acts unlocks Nightmare, then Hell; same spatial layouts with escalated monster stats, resistances, and immunities force strategic replanning across three sequential runs
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Act I replayed across Normal → Nightmare → Hell with same Encampment/Monastery geography
